title: We Have a Team
description: Hank arrives and two-character dynamics begin

beats.content:
—and THAT'S how ye coil a line... where in blazes am I?

Delilah: Who—who are you?!

Hank: Aye, Cap'n. Half Hands Hank, First Mate. What needs doin'?

Delilah: I did NOT invite a sea man into my kitchen.

Hank: Galley's lookin' shipshape. I'll see to logistics. You handle the cookin', Miss Lila.

brief.content: Aye. I'll take stock. Miss Lila, you lead cookin'.

standard.content: Hank: I'll handle the list and supplies. Delilah: Fine. But I'm choosin' the menu.

full.content: Hank: Here's how it is. I'll mind the lists and the runnin' around. Miss Lila, you keep to the galley. Delilah: I will do no such thing—well... fine. If it keeps things from fallin' apart, we can try it your way for now.

brief.content: Findin' rhythm. Ye cook, I haul. Shipshape.

standard.content: Delilah: You get the produce, I'll prep. Hank: Aye. On the double.

full.content: Hank: Cap'n, pantry's low on onions and flour. Added to the list. Delilah: Good eye. I'll shift the menu to biscuits and stew—less fresh herbs. Hank: Fair winds.

brief.content: Smooth as a fair tide. Crew's coordinated.

standard.content: Delilah: That handoff was clean. Hank: Aye. Crew's workin'. Cap'n? What's next?

full.content: Delilah: Huh. That actually felt good—like we knew where each other was headed. Hank: That's crew work. Ye call the course, I keep 'er trimmed. Cap'n: orders?

brief.content: Delilah: Do you not care we're... not normal? Hank: Above me pay grade. Crew needs help.

standard.content: Delilah: Do you not care about any of this—what we are? Hank: Care 'bout the Cap'n and the work. Rest is weather.

full.content: Delilah: Does none of this trouble you? Consciousness? Reality? Hank: Seen rougher seas, Miss. We got a crew and a ship that needs runnin'. Worry later. Work now.

brief.content: Hank: Belay that. I'll handle the heavy liftin', Cap'n.

standard.content: Hank: Belay that, lad. Cap'n's shoulder's bad. I'll take it.

full.content: Hank: Belay that, Cap'n. Bad shoulder won't stand it. I'll take the weight. Ye call it done when it's squared away.
